description: One of my favorite things about Bali are the affordable spa services. You can get a one-hour aromatherapy massage for as little as 80,000 IDR ($6 USD)!

Here's what it was like staying at the AYANA Resort and Spa in Bali.

The AYANA Resort and Spa BALI has a prime cliff-top location over Jimbaran Bay, just six miles from the airport. While it feels a million miles from anywhere, it’s actually a really central spot. The resort has 290 chic rooms, 12 swimming pools, and 19 restaurants. I stayed in an ocean view suite at AYANA and the view of the Indian Ocean was incredible. A massive space full of little luxury touches, it felt more like a home than a hotel room.

Alchemy is one of the most well-known vegan eateries in Ubud and is the first 100% raw vegan restaurant in Bali.

Alchemy is one of the most well-known vegan eateries in Ubud and is the first 100% raw vegan restaurant in Bali. Alchemy is a gorgeous restaurant/cafe/shop, which features a giant make-your-own-salad bar and large fully raw menu. Food options include items such as raw pizza, vegan sushi, miso soup, and vegan nachos. Alchemy also offers delicious sweet treats, such as raw vegan cheesecake and vegan chocolate truffles, as well as a variety of homemade beverages.

One of the lesser visited spots around Bali, the Aling Aling waterfall is a great place to explore.

Some of the lesser visited spots around Bali include the Aling Aling waterfall
Ametis Villa An impressive luxury pick, these villas are only a three-minute walk from Batu Bolong Beach. With a woodsy-chic style, every room here is dreamy.

Annupuri Villas Bali If you’re going for ambiance, look no further than Annupuri Villas! The affordable resort has a restaurant, pool, bar, and garden—plus you’ll feel submerged in the jungle while you’re here!
For incredible seafood and a sunset that you’ll never forget, make reservations at Kisik at the Ayana Resort for a sunset dinner. Arrive early for a pre-dinner drink at the cliff-faced Rock Bar.
See Indonesia’s most beautiful dining room housed inside the 300-year-old Bale Sutra temple and take in the otherworldly beauty while you eat world-class food.

Bali is an incredible destination for all kinds of travelers. Whether you’re a solo female traveler backpacking your way around Southeast Asia or you’re looking to unwind at yoga and surf retreats, there’s something for everyone.

Bali, as you may know, is a beautiful and lively island located in the Asian country of Indonesia. It is known for its surf beaches, breathtaking rice terraces, lush jungles and AMAZING VEGAN FOOD!

Bali is mentioned as a starting point for flights to Labuan Bajo, with seven flights a day from Denpasar.

The sunrise hike up Mount Batur (Bara Batur) is famous in Bali. Locally considered to be a sacred mountain, Mount Batur is an active volcano. It’s totally safe, though, and a must-see for any nature lover.

Batu Bolong was the author's favorite spot, with countless fish and stunning coral, making it the most epic diving experience they've ever had.

If you’re into surfing, the main road in town, Batu Bolong, is the place to be.
Batu Bolong is good for surfers of all levels.
Berawa is good for surfers of all levels.
Honorable Mentions
Biliq CoSharing Space is a coworking space with indoor and outdoor spaces, a cafe, zen rooms, and a pool. They offer a day pass starting at $11.

Located in Canggu and within a short walk to Echo Beach, this hostel has a beautiful pool and offers a restaurant, free WiFi, an outdoor swimming pool, plus garden and pool view terraces. Beds start as low as $9 USD per night.
Honorable Mentions

One of the most popular temples in Yogyakarta and considered one of the greatest Buddhist monuments in the world. It's shrouded in Indonesian myths and legends, so I'd highly recommend hiring a guide to learn more about the history of the place!

Located in the Kedu Valley, on the island of Java, you’ll find one of the greatest Buddhist monuments in the world as well as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built in the 8th and 9th centuries AD, the compound contains three main temples, Borobudur Temple being the largest, and 72 stupas each with a statue of Buddha.
snorkeling or diving at Bunaken Island, North Sulawesi. Fly into Manado, and speedboat.
One of my favorite spots in Seminyak for a lazy brunch on a budget is Cafe Bali. Shabby chic decor and a delicious breakfast make the perfect setting for a fresh start to the day.

Cafe Organic is a restaurant which includes a wide variety of vegan breakfasts, appetizers, burgers, salads, smoothie bowls and more.

Cafe Organic is very popular with the breakfast crowd and offers amazing food, great coffee, friendly staff, and beautiful decor.

Cafe Organic This cafe serves amazing breakfast and lunch all day, as well as fresh smoothies.

Great sunsets? Check. Beautiful beaches? Check. Beautiful, expansive, rice fields? Check. Canggu is one of our favorite villages in Bali

There’s a reason why surfers love Bali. It’s got flawless weather and some of the most consistent surf on the planet. If you know the basics and are looking for a quiet, less touristy place to surf, look no further than Canggu.

Everything is super close together and it’s easy to walk around. It’s also a very safe area so it is one of my first recommendations for solo female travelers in Bali. For this very reason, you will also be able to meet other travelers too. There are lots of laid-back beach cafés and bars. If you’re into surfing, the main road in town, Batu Bolong, is the place to be. Keep your eye out for quiet little warungs (roadside cafés) all over and some of the coolest street art!
Located on Canggu Beach (a popular surf beach with lots of cute boutiques and cafes), the hotel is in a great place to learn to surf or watch the sunset.
A commenter asked about diving at Cannibal Rock, but the author did not visit this site.
Honorable Mentions
Staying at Coconut Garden Beach Resort in Indonesia
Colabo Coworking is a coworking space with indoor and outdoor spaces, onsite yoga, a venue for events, Zumba classes, and more. They offer a day pass for $11.

Crate Cafe Full of unique and healthy picks, this cafe is a foodie’s heaven.

Honorable Mentions
DAVA is a restaurant at the AYANA Resort and Spa BALI, specializing in steak and ultra fresh seafood. It’s a great spot for dinner.
Denpasar is the city in Bali where flights to Labuan Bajo depart from.
Deus Ex Machina Cafe, Nightlife This café is located between the Temple of Enthusiasm and the rice paddies of Canggu and is a wonderful fusion of Indonesian culture and Western cuisine.

Dojo Canggu is a coworking space located next to Echo Beach, with a two-story space, art facilities, a pool, tropical gardens, and booths for professionals. They host events, workshops, community BBQs, and social meetups. Amenities include a smoothie shack, Nalu Bowls, and a Skype Booth with AC.

Doka Village, located outside of Maumare, is another top spot to take in the local Indonesian culture. The village is known for its friendly locals, and many travelers explore the village to check out the local handicrafts.

Earth Cafe boasts page after page of unbelievable vegan dishes in its large menu. The only thing that I dislike about Earth Cafe is deciding what to order.

Earth Cafe boasts page after page of unbelievable vegan dishes in its large menu. The only thing that I dislike about Earth Cafe is deciding what to order. With options such as build-your-own bowls, vegan burgers, soups/curries, sandwiches/wraps, falafel, Indian dishes, vegan tacos and more, one can feel overwhelmed (in a good way) with all of the delicious options. Earth Cafe also features a ton of delectable desserts, tasty smoothies, juices coffee and teas.
My fave areas to explore are the quirkier areas of Jalan Petitenget and Jalan Kayu Aya as well as the popular Eat Street.

Located in Canggu and within a short walk to Echo Beach, this hostel has a beautiful pool and offers a restaurant, free WiFi, an outdoor swimming pool, plus garden and pool view terraces.

Echo Beach is a beach located next to Dojo Canggu, where you can watch surfers or take a ride yourself.

Spend a lazy afternoon enjoying the beauty of Echo Beach.
The area surrounding Ende is famous for its ikat weaving, in particular, the village of Ngella is renowned for its textiles.
Finn's Beach Club This beach club offers beachfront daybeds, 2 pools with live DJs daily, 6 bars, a restaurant and spa, all in one location!
Known for its holistic treatments, with 90-minute sessions starting at IDR 1,550,000, around $100 USD.

Flores Island is known for its growing ecotourism industry, with traditional villages and diverse native wildlife to explore. The island is also home to the famous crater lakes surrounding Mount Kelimutu.

The island of Flores is more than just natural beauty. You’ll want to check out the urban areas of Flores too.

An alluring archipelago that boasts a fascinating history, otherworldly landscapes, and hair-raising adventures. It's the perfect destination in Indonesia to wander off the beaten path and get a serious adrenaline rush! Spend your trip conquering challenging hikes past active volcanoes, diving some of Indonesia's top dive sites, and learning about the real-life Hobbits who apparently lived on the island hundreds of years ago.
Which Four Seasons Bali Resort is Better After staying in both Four Seasons Bali resorts, at Jimbaran Bay and at Sayan- I have a lot to share about both, amazing experiences!

Ready for the most romantic dinner of your life? Honestly, this dinner was proposal worthy. Other than your server you will not see another human. You and your partner will be sitting in privacy in the tree tops surrounded by a beautiful lotus pond.
Named after the Sanskrit word for 'beautiful,' Sundara is situated in the luxurious Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very handy if you’re planning to stay at this five-star resort.
Gili Air is the most “local” island; providing the most grassroots feel of the three

The Gili Islands are another excellent option if you’re looking to spend longer than a day away.

Ok so technically they’re a separate set of islands in Indonesia, but the Gili Islands must be precisely what the first honeymooners ever had in mind. Made up of three islands, they are a true paradise.

If you have a few extra days during your trip, I highly recommend catching a ferry to the neighboring islands of Lombok and the Gili Islands —I visited both solo, for a few days of scuba diving, and it was paradise!
Gili Meno is the honeymoon island

My personal favorite is Gili Trawangan! It’s the perfect destination for scuba divers or travelers looking to experience Gili’s legendary nightlife!

Gili Trawangan (aka Gili “T”) is the party island

A small island located off the coast of Bali and is less than six square miles in total, making it easy to rent a bicycle or walk to anywhere you want to go! With direct ferries available every day for only a couple of dollars, you can swap the crowds of Bali for a few days of the slow and simple life here. You can unwind on the beautiful beaches with a book or head out into the ocean. The snorkeling and diving conditions are top-notch, and Gili T is where you'll find that Insta-famous swing in the ocean!

This modern restaurant is spread over two levels and produces dishes that are perfect for sharing.

For quality meals, make sure you visit the popular Seminyak venue, Ginger Moon. This modern restaurant is spread over two levels and produces dishes that are perfect for sharing.

Offers a range of treatments in a chic setting. A 60-minute relaxation massage starts at IDR 320,000 (Indonesian Rupiah), which is approximately $19.69 USD.

Goldust Beauty Lounge I HIGHLY recommend everything it’s got!

This budget hotel offers modern, single rooms starting at $46 USD per night.

Just a 2-minute walk from Seminyak Beach, this budget hotel offers modern, cozy rooms starting at $40 USD per night.
Experience a range of Balinese treatments, including their signature hot bamboo massage for IDR 350,000.

The hotel is dizzying-ly beautiful and a far cry from the cookie-cutter properties you’ll find everywhere else. The owner’s mind-blowing collection of Indonesian art and antiques is housed exclusively in his gorgeous hotels. Every room in Tugu Bali is unique, from the massive Puri Le Mayeur Villa set atop a pond to the Walter Spies Pavilion, which calls back to Java’s art deco era in the early twentieth century. Each decorated with unique artwork, the Dedari, and Rejang suites feature oversized beds, sunken baths, private pools, and in-room spas. My favorite part, each room is nestled in private gardens and offers spectacular views of rice paddy fields and panoramic sunsets! Whichever option you go for, you can look forward to a complimentary welcome massage and daily fresh tropical fruits and flowers.

Hotel Tugu Bali Another luxe site located steps away from the beach and popular Canggu bars, this posh, vintage spot has soaking tubs and private plunge pools.

Hubud is a coworking and coliving space made of repurposed bamboo, with high-speed WiFi, bamboo meeting rooms, events and workshops, storage lockers, a healthy cafe, Skype booths, and 24/7 access. Membership options include unlimited access, 30 hours, or a day pass.
Indigo Canggu Japanese This modern Japanese restaurant offers creative dishes, and amazing seafood, in an intimate setting.
My fave areas to explore are the quirkier areas of Jalan Petitenget and Jalan Kayu Aya as well as the popular Eat Street.
My fave areas to explore are the quirkier areas of Jalan Petitenget and Jalan Kayu Aya as well as the popular Eat Street.

One of the lesser visited spots around Bali, the Jatiluwih rice terraces are a great place to explore.

Some of the lesser visited spots around Bali include the Jatiluwih rice terraces

My favorite meal was dinner on the rooftop at Ji, their Japanese Fusion & Sake Bar! I highly recommend going for happy hour and getting the Coco Sexo cocktail- not only is it delicious but it comes out bubbling and smoking like a magical brew!

It is a lovely mix of a luxury resort with local communities. The water is calm in the bay and there are plenty of spots to try ultra-fresh seafood or just lounge by the water. For an afternoon on the town, browse the local market. While Jimbaran Bay can get a bit crowded, it isn’t as touristy as some other spots.

Jimbaran Bay is a bay located on Bali's west coast, known for its luxury resorts, seafood, and beaches.

Jimbaran Bay is a beautiful beach with crystal clear water, located near the AYANA Resort and Spa BALI.

Lined with seafood restaurants, a couple of 5-star resorts, and the famous Jimbaran Fish Market, Jimbaran Beach and Jimbaran Bay encompass all the things Bali is all about: good food, good people, beautiful beaches, and local culture.
There are countless shops lined up on Jl. Soekarno Hatta road, near the Le Pirate Resort, offering the same day tour for a fraction of the cost.

The biggest highlight of my trip to Flores Island was visiting the Kelimutu Volcanic Crater Lakes. Thanks to a chemical reaction from some nearby volcanoes, the waters of the three lakes change colors!

The color-changing Kelimutu Volcanic Crater Lakes, located on the island of Flores , is another must-see. Thanks to a chemical reaction from nearby volcano gas activity, these three iconic lakes can be aqua, emerald, chocolate, or ruby red.
A secluded beach on Nusa Penida that's famous for its stunning views and crystal-clear waters. It's a must-visit for any beach lover, and it's a great spot for snorkeling and diving.

Kinship Studio is a modern coworking space with private offices, communal workspace, a photography studio, photography equipment rentals, a cafe, weekly yoga, and workshops. They offer a day pass or a monthly pass with access to all amenities.

Kismet is a funky and beautiful restaurant/bar which offers many vegan-friendly options. Choose one of Kismet’s masterpiece salad bowls or charcoal-bun burgers.

Kismet is a funky and beautiful restaurant/bar which offers many vegan-friendly options. Choose one of Kismet’s masterpiece salad bowls or charcoal-bun burgers. Kismet also offers vegan sandwiches, wraps, soups, appetizers, veggie sushi, pasta dishes, and desserts. I can personally recommend the 666 Southwest Bowl, which features spicy marinated jackfruit and an avocado-corn tartare.
Komodo Dive Center is a dive company that offers guided dives in Komodo National Park.

The Komodo Islands are home to more marine diversity than any other area on the planet, with crystal clear seas surrounding the islands. The islands are perfect for beach lovers and divers alike, with gorgeous coral and an impressive array of underwater life.

If you love animals, search for the world’s largest lizards at UNESCO World Heritage Site Komodo Island.

Home to over 5,000 of the world's largest lizards: the Komodo dragon. These critters can grow up to 8 feet long and weigh more than 200 pounds! Komodo National Park is a must-visit for any nature lover. With incredible diving, beautiful islands, pink beaches, and even forests, it's one of my favorite destinations in all of Indo.

Komodo National Park is made up of three large islands: Komodo, Rinca, and Padar, providing a dramatic landscape to the lush park. The park is full of sandy beaches and sparkling water, making it the perfect place to explore the natural beauty of the country.

The natural beauty of Flores Island is truly incredible. There are few places as remarkable as Indonesia’s amazing Komodo National Park. Plan to spend at least one day exploring the area.

A must-visit for any nature lover. With incredible diving, beautiful islands, pink beaches, and even forests, it's one of my favorite destinations in all of Indo.

This is a very trendy and design-centric hostel — it has a beautiful pool and a stunning buffet breakfast. A dorm bed starts at $25 USD per night.

This is a very trendy and design-centric hostel — it has a beautiful pool and a stunning buffet breakfast.

Amanda Barber mentioned that she is not into the party scene so she doesn’t think Kuta would be for her

Known for the backpacker crowd, you can find a cheap place to stay here BUT Bali is not an expensive travel destination. There’s no sense in staying in a crazy area to save a couple of dollars. The tourist packs can be overwhelming here compared to the more chill backpacker vibe you will find in Canggu or Seminyak.

Kuta, the main town of South Lombok, has blossomed and it's the perfect spot for your next vacation.
Relax on the beach in Kuta, Seminyak, or Canggu

On my most recent trip, I visited La Laguna in Canggu and was blown away by the beautiful beach, amazing sunset, and lively nightlife.

La Laguna Seafood, Bar This gypsy-inspired “rustic chic” spot has a beachfront bar and a restaurant you will definitely want to try.

La Pacha Mama is the choice for vegan-Mexican. La Pacha Mama features an all-plant-based menu for its vegan guests.

If you are in the mood for vegan-Mexican, then La Pacha Mama is the choice for you! La Pacha Mama features an all-plant-based menu for its vegan guests. Choose from delicious dishes such as coconut tostadas, beet carpaccio, vegan burritos or shiitake tacos. La Pacha Mama also features a vegetarian menu, from which you can also create vegan options—simply ask for no cheese and/or no sour cream, and you can find additional vegan dishes such as nachos and quesadillas.
La Plancha has quirky decor, a funky 'Spanish meets surfer' style, and fantastic Mediterranean-inspired cuisine for those wanting something to nibble on.

Labuan Bajo is the gateway to Komodo National Park and is becoming a boom town, with a growing ecotourism industry.

Once a quiet fishing village, Labuan Bajo has become a thriving tourist center. While travelers don’t spend a ton of time in Labuan Bajo itself, the city is the perfect base for exploring Komodo National Park and the island of Flores.

The gateway to Komodo Island. Fly into Labuan Bajo and sign up for a day tour to get the most out of a trip to Komodo Island.
It's super cute and in a great location. There are lots of places around Labuan Bajo where you can get a glimpse into the culture of the local Manggarai people.

Le Pirate Resort is the perfect base in Labuan Bajo. With just fourteen rooms, a rooftop sky bar, live music, an outdoor cinema, and an amazing pool, this place was designed with the young, modern traveler in mind.
Visit Lempuyang Temple (entrance is under $5 USD)

A small island off the coast of Bali that deserves a spot on any Indonesia itinerary. I'd suggest basing yourself in South Lombok and planning day trips all over the islands. Some of my favorite parts about this island are the beaches and waterfalls! Start your adventure off with a hike through the jungle to Sendang Gile Waterfall and end your day off at Tanjung Aan beach.

If you have a few extra days during your trip, I highly recommend catching a ferry to the neighboring islands of Lombok and the Gili Islands —I visited both solo, for a few days of scuba diving, and it was paradise!
A commenter mentioned diving at Manta Alley and seeing about 35 mantas, describing it as their favorite dive of all time due to the playful nature and proximity of the mantas.

Manta Point is a dive site where you can see manta rays, although the author missed them during their visit. The site is still super interesting to explore.

If you love animals, swim with the mantas while you snorkel, or dive Manta Point, and then search for the world’s largest lizards at UNESCO World Heritage Site Komodo Island.

The best diving and snorkeling in Indonesia are in areas like Manta Point, that offer a truly magical experience. We opted to organize a private tour that included diving instead of snorkeling, but the standard tours will include snorkeling, which I highly recommend! While you might not see mantas every time, you will get to see a gorgeous array of coral and sea life.
Most day tours from Labuan Bajo include a visit to Padar, lunch at Pink Beach, a visit to Komodo Island and snorkeling at Manta Ray Point.
Doka Village, located outside of Maumare, is another top spot to take in the local Indonesian culture.
Milk & Madu European, Cafe For a European-style breakfast and lunch, this is a great café.
Honorable Mentions

Smack-dab in the jungle, Ubud is known for its forest home to hundreds of monkeys, but be careful! Some of the monkeys can be super aggressive towards tourists – especially if you make eye contact and don’t have any food.

Visit Ubud Monkey Forest (around $5 – $8 USD entry)

Close to the Monkey Forest, this is the lush region that you’ve definitely seen featured on Instagram from time to time.

Mount Kelimutu is home to the famous crater lakes, which change color due to chemical reactions caused by volcano gas activity. The lakes are ultra-photogenic and can be found in aqua, chocolate brown, green, and red colors.

One of my favorite spots in all of Indonesia is Mount Kelimutu. This is on the other side of Flores, so you’ll need to take a short flight to get there. Arrive before sunrise to watch the volcanic lakes change color!
Popular clubs like Mrs. Sippy's and Potato Head Beach Club are located in Seminyak and give you fun swim-up bars, infinity pools, a variety of day beds, and lots of DJs and entertainment.

For tours around the island, hire a local driver and split the cost with your friends or other travelers to save money. You can hire a driver for less than 300,000 IDR ($23 USD) for the day and visit sites like Mt. Batur.

Hire a local driver for the day, and split the cost with fellow travelers to save money, and visit sites like Ubud Monkey Forest, Mt. Batur, Uluwatu, and the Pura Gunung Kawi Temple.

One of the most romantic things we did was a sunrise hike of Mt. Batur. Barring clouds, you’ll see an incredible night sky just before the sunrise graces you with a beautiful array of colors over the volcano.

An active volcano that's a must-visit for any adventure-seeker. You can hike to the summit and watch the sunrise over the surrounding landscape. The views are breathtaking, and it's an experience you'll never forget.
Honorable Mentions
The area surrounding Ende is famous for its ikat weaving, in particular, the village of Ngella is renowned for its textiles.
Nude Canggu Cafe For fresh foods and all things healthy, Nude Canggu is the place for vegetarians, vegans, and anyone looking for a light meal.
Amanda Barber is planning to visit Nusa Dua for 3 days

A string of three islands off the coast of Bali. Some call them 'The Maldives of Bali,' and it's not hard to see why. Nusa Penida is famous for its secluded Kelingking Beach, Nusa Lembongan's highlight is the Devil's Tear, and Nusa Ceningan attracts thrill-seekers with its epic cliff jumping. But all three islands offer exceptional diving or snorkeling conditions and a variety of accommodation options to suit any budget.
Nusa Penida is a relatively underdeveloped island located south-east of Bali. While it lacks the trendy restaurants and beach clubs, you will find incredible natural beauty!
Old Man's for Nightlife For a fun night out, check out the drink, food, and music at this local hot spot.
Offers a range of treatments, including aromatherapy massages starting at IDR 950,000 (approximately $60 USD).

Ora Dive is a dive company that offers guided dives in Komodo National Park.

I went with Ora Dive — they offer custom tours and packaged day trips so you can choose what you'd like to see the most! Our private tour was organized through Ora Dive and it was a great experience, but rather expensive since we chose a private charter with diving.
Oshan is a luxurious collection of villas and rooms that feature a beautiful pool, a landscaped garden, and overlook scenic rice fields!

Outpost Canggu is a coworking space with an open-air workspace, a sun-soaked terrace, an on-site pool, and an on-site barista. They offer a day pass or packages with hours ranging from 30 to unlimited.

Outpost Ubud is a coworking space with a large air-conditioned focus room, a balcony with ravine views, meeting rooms, a garden space, a kitchen, and a jungle pool. Amenities include high-speed internet, 24/7 access, a swimming pool, Skype booths, and a printer and scanner. They also offer jungle-chic boutique villas and studios for overnight stays.
Padang Padang Left is a popular spot for surfers, with epic waves and a stunning location.
Padang Padang Right is a popular spot for surfers, with epic waves and a stunning location. It’s recommended for newbies and intermediates.

The view from the peaks of Padar Island is the most beautiful sight in Indonesia. Climb your way to the top for a panorama view of the incredible natural beauty of Komodo National Park. Don't forget lots of sunscreen and a hat — it's a hot and sunny adventure to the top. The hike is a bit of a challenge but it's worth it.

For some breathtaking vistas, grab your hiking shoes and head to Padar Island for some of the best views you’ll ever see.

The third largest island inside Komodo National Park. It is the place to go in Indonesia if you're an avid hiker and photographer. There are challenging trails that will take you to the top of the island's tallest peaks for a breathtaking view of the surrounding area! Plus, you can easily incorporate a visit to Padar Island with your day trip to Pink Beach, Manta Ray, and Komodo Island by joining a tour.

The third largest island in Komodo National Park, Padar Island is another great place for a hike. While the paths are a bit more challenging than Bara Batur’s, they are definitely worth exploring.
Beautiful cliffs along the coast meet white sand and crystal clear blue water. Pandawa also has wonderful surf, but be careful because the wave can be a bit heavy and the reef can be a bit shallow.

Peloton Supershop is vegan heaven! This 100% plant-based restaurant makes for the perfect breakfast spot and features all of the classics, including avocado toast, smoothie bowls, and breakfast burritos.

Peloton Supershop Cafe, Nightlife This combo vegan café and bicycle shop is all about eco-friendly living.

Peloton Supershop is vegan heaven! This 100% plant-based restaurant makes for the perfect breakfast spot and features all of the classics, including avocado toast, smoothie bowls, and breakfast burritos. Peloton’s diverse lunch/dinner menu items feature meals such as jackfruit tacos, superfood power bowls, green Thai noodle curry, veggie burgers, and vegan lasagna!
If you’re into surfing be sure to check out Petitenget and Seminyak beaches.

Pink Beach was actually very green and murky the day we went, but the coral was still incredible.

This beautiful, Instagram-ready beach is home to lush, powder-pink sand.

Most day tours from Labuan Bajo include a visit to Padar, lunch at Pink Beach, a visit to Komodo Island and snorkeling at Manta Ray Point.
Amazing food, great service, such a cute venue and really affordable. Also a great place to bring your laptop and work from!
Popular clubs like Mrs. Sippy's and Potato Head Beach Club are located in Seminyak and give you fun swim-up bars, infinity pools, a variety of day beds, and lots of DJs and entertainment.

One of the most popular temples in Yogyakarta and considered one of the greatest Buddhist monuments in the world. It's shrouded in Indonesian myths and legends, so I'd highly recommend hiring a guide to learn more about the history of the place!

For tours around the island, hire a local driver and split the cost with your friends or other travelers to save money. You can hire a driver for less than 300,000 IDR ($23 USD) for the day and visit sites like the Pura Gunung Kawi Temple.

Hire a local driver for the day, and split the cost with fellow travelers to save money, and visit sites like Ubud Monkey Forest, Mt. Batur, Uluwatu, and the Pura Gunung Kawi Temple.
Built to honor Dewi Saraswati, the goddess of wisdom and the arts, this beautiful temple is alive with lotus blossoms and water flows.
RIMBA Jimbaran BALI by AYANA is a nearby sister property of the AYANA Resort and Spa BALI, with a lovely Sunday brunch and a Rooftop Bar & Restaurant with Mexican food and cocktails.

If you only have time for one destination on this list, bump Raja Ampat to the top of your must-see list. Last year, I spent 12 days on a diving liveaboard boat trip through Raja Ampat, and it's one of my all-time favorite travel experiences! You'll get to travel to remote islands, dive in pristine conditions, and explore dive sites that are not otherwise accessible.

This year I spent 12 days on diving liveaboard boat through Raja Ampat and the Banda Sea! This is an incredible way to connect with the ocean, see remote islands, and get some real dive experience under your belt.
The Rock Bar is a must-see spot at the AYANA Resort and Spa BALI, with a striking sunset view and tasty tapas and Mediterranean food.
A simple B&B with air-conditioned rooms and free WiFi, and close to the Ubud Palace and Monkey Forest. Double rooms start at $14 USD per night!

Sanur was our home base for a majority of our time in Bali. Sanur is widely known as the honeymoon village due to its quiet nature, cute street shops, beautiful beaches, and world-class resorts.

This is a family-oriented spot without a lot to do. It has a rather sleepy reputation and it can take quite a bit of time to get to other places. While there are resorts with nice places to relax, the main beaches aren’t very well maintained.
Honorable Mentions
Satu Satu Coffee Company Cafe, Coffee For some of the best coffee on the island, as well as tasty vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free noshes, get to Satu Satu.

Sayuri Healing Foods believes in the healing power of food and understands that food is energy (we are what we eat). Every dish at Sayuri is created with intention and serves to nourish the body.

Sayuri Healing Foods believes in the healing power of food and understands that food is energy (we are what we eat). Every dish at Sayuri is created with intention and serves to nourish the body. Sayuri offers predominately high-vibe raw food but also prepares deliciously cooked dishes which incorporate the wisdom of Ayurveda and Ancient Chinese nutrition. Sayuri is meat-free, dairy-free and wheat-free.

Seminyak is one of the popular areas in Bali.

Bali is more than Seminyak and Ubud.

Looking for a swanky, upscale spot on the sand? You are going to love Seminyak. This is one of the biggest beach communities and you will have everything outside of your doorstep. Luxe resorts, high-end shopping, and sleek bars are the standard here. My fave areas to explore are the quirkier areas of Jalan Petitenget and Jalan Kayu Aya as well as the popular Eat Street. If you’re into surfing be sure to check out Petitenget and Seminyak beaches.

Just a 2-minute walk from Seminyak Beach, this budget hotel offers modern, single rooms starting at $46 USD per night.

If you’re into surfing be sure to check out Petitenget and Seminyak beaches.

Plan to spend at least one afternoon exploring the amazing natural beauty of the region. Start by trekking though the lush green forest to Sendang Gile Waterfall, and then hike on to Tiu Kelep Waterfall where you can go for a refreshing swim.
Honorable Mentions
Shore Amora Canggu Offering suites, rooms, and pool villas, all with stunning views, this is a beautiful design hotel located in the heart of Canggu.
Honorable Mentions
Set amidst lush greenery, their 60-minute relaxing Balinese massage is priced at IDR 1,160,000 (about $72 USD).
This Bed & Breakfast features an outdoor pool and private rooms starting at $28 USD. Daily breakfast is served on the private terrace overlooking the garden.

One of the lesser visited spots around Bali, the Suluban Beach Cave is a great place to explore.

Some of the lesser visited spots around Bali include the Suluban Beach Cave

The best place to visit in Indonesia if you're after exceptional wildlife experiences. It's the archipelago's largest island, and it's where you can find wild orangutans and the Sumatran tiger! The tigers are the hardest to spot, but you can book hikes in Bukit Lawang where you'll be able to see orangutans in their natural habitat. For divers, you'll be spoiled for choice! There are dozens of islands and atolls dotting the coastline—Pulau Meh is considered to be one of the best places in the world to go scuba diving!

Sundara is situated in the luxurious Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very handy if you’re planning to stay at this five-star resort. Patrons can listen to the sound of the ocean lapping on the nearby beach as they dine, as well as take in the spectacular view of Jimbaran Bay.

Food lovers can head to one of the region’s more recent hotspots, Sundara. Named after the Sanskrit word for 'beautiful,' Sundara is situated in the luxurious Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very handy if you’re planning to stay at this five-star resort.
Honorable Mentions

Tanah Lot is a stunning rock temple off the coast—you won’t be able to go into the main temple at Tanah Lot, but I think that it is worth a visit if you’re interested in stunning coastal views and Balinese culture.

Located twenty minutes north of Seminyak, Canggu spans from just north of Seminyak to south of Tanah Lot.
When you visit Canggu, be sure to visit Tanah Lot temple!

Explore Tegallalang Rice Terraces (small donation/entry fee)

The best place to get a view is at the world-famous Tegallalang Rice Terraces in Ubud . The Balinese use a unique system of irrigation that gives their rice fields a distinct beauty.

Odds are you’ve seen pics of the beautiful Tegalalang rice terraces before. Travelers often pop over to take a few photos and spend a night or two. I’d recommend sticking around for a bit longer though in this underrated area. Wherever you stay, get up early at least one morning for a sunrise hike up Mt. Batur!

Swim at waterfalls like Tegenungan or Tibumana ($1–3 USD)

Want to feel like you’re in a scene out of a romantic movie? Head over to the Tegenungan Waterfall and swim in the water underneath the waterfall with your loved one.
Honorable Mentions
The Lawn This beachfront restaurant is a must-see. It’s an idyllic locale to hang out on a sunny afternoon or sip coconut mojitos at night.

The Nest Canggu Hostel Steps away from Batu Bolong beach, The Nest is a popular hostel choice for travelers. The hostel features an outdoor swimming pool and a shared kitchen and free WiFi throughout the property.

The Seeds of Life Cafe is my personal favorite restaurant in all of Bali. The Seeds of Life redefines raw vegan cuisine and features a fully-raw menu with a variety of enticing options for any breakfast lunch or dinner.

The Seeds of Life redefines raw vegan cuisine and features a fully-raw menu with a variety of enticing options for any breakfast lunch or dinner. The signature dish at Seeds of Life has to be the SOL Bowl, and I highly recommend that you give it a try. The Sol Bowl features a surprising combination of interesting ingredients including coconut jerky, homemade hummus, nori paper, kimchi, tempeh and fresh spirulina atop a bowl of greens and veggies. In addition to its amazing food choices, guests can enjoy one of the many tasty elixirs, juices or teas served in SOL’s tonic bar.

The Shady Shack is a breezy, open-air cafe/restaurant with food which is just as healthy as it is delicious.

The Shady Shack is a breezy, open-air cafe/restaurant with food which is just as healthy as it is delicious. All-day breakfast, power bowls, vegan burgers and sharing plates make The Shady Shack an excellent choice no matter your level of hunger or food cravings. Enjoy the food as well as the beautiful location!

The Spell Creperie is a magical, treehouse-like eatery known as The Spell. The Spell is known for its mouth-watering sweet crepes and savory galletas, which come in just about every flavor combo you can imagine.

The Spell is known for its mouth-watering sweet crepes and savory galletas, which come in just about every flavor combo you can imagine. The Spell serves up savory crepes featuring fillings such as marinated jackfruit, vegan foie gras, vegan bolognese, sautéed mushrooms/onions, vegan cheese, Balinese veggies and more. The Spell’s sweet crepes are the perfect choice for a yummy breakfast or dessert. Sweet filling choices include homemade caramel, vegan chocolate sauce, coconut whipped cream and of course a variety of tropical, local fruits.

The Villas at AYANA offer a more exclusive and intimate experience, with 78 massive villas with private pools and 24-hour butler service. This place is luxe, with a 5-star rating from Forbes Travel Guide.

Also highly recommended in Ubud is The Yoga Barn, a place to stretch the body, clear the mind, and take in stunning scenery.

Also highly recommended in Ubud is The Yoga Barn, a place to stretch the body, clear the mind, and take in stunning scenery. The facility provides a large number of yoga classes (many of which are free) in styles such as Iyengar, Hatha, Vinyasa Flow, Pranayama, Power, and Yin.
Swim at waterfalls like Tegenungan or Tibumana ($1–3 USD)

Plan to spend at least one afternoon exploring the amazing natural beauty of the region. Start by trekking though the lush green forest to Sendang Gile Waterfall, and then hike on to Tiu Kelep Waterfall where you can go for a refreshing swim.

Tropical Nomad is a coworking space with high-speed internet, private meeting rooms, balconies with jungle views, Skype booths, mail service, and more. They offer a one-day trial pass, and unlimited monthly access. Amenities include a free drink and food with the trial pass.
Tugu Spa I’m also a big fan of the Tugu Spa.

Tugu's Restaurant Ready for a fancy dinner out? Travel back to ancient Indonesia for a romantic adventure through the history of the country as you explore the artifacts and artwork at Hotel Tugu, and then sample authentic, Balinese fare.
Uber Scuba is a dive company that offers guided dives in Komodo National Park.

Ubud is known for its forest home to hundreds of monkeys, but be careful! Some of the monkeys can be super aggressive towards tourists – especially if you make eye contact and don’t have any food.

Everything about Ubud emanates absolute passion and romance. The foundation of the city is built upon the mind-blowing rice fields, handcrafted local Balinese coffee, and custom jewelry and art.

The best place to get a view is at the world-famous Tegallalang Rice Terraces in Ubud . The Balinese use a unique system of irrigation that gives their rice fields a distinct beauty.

A simple B&B with air-conditioned rooms and free WiFi, and close to the Ubud Palace and Monkey Forest.

A simple B&B with air-conditioned rooms and free WiFi, and close to the Ubud Palace and Monkey Forest. Double rooms start at $13 USD per night!
Ulekan Bali Ulekan is MUST when visiting Bali—it offers seriously delicious, authentic Indonesian food.

Hire a local driver for the day, and split the cost with fellow travelers to save money, and visit sites like Ubud Monkey Forest, Mt. Batur, Uluwatu, and the Pura Gunung Kawi Temple.

Uluwatu exudes romance. In the two months we visited Bali, we traveled just about the entire island and agree that Uluwatu was our favorite village we visited.

Famous for its insanely good surfing and the Pura Luhur Uluwatu Temple , Uluwatu is one of the best places in Bali to watch the sunset. Explore the area solo, or check out one of the local tours that will take you to watch the sunset over the Ulwuatu temple.

From the Uluwatu Temple to the beaches and on to the cliffs, Uluwatu exudes romance.

Plan a day to walk up to Uluwatu Temple for a panoramic view.

Uluwatu Temple, also known as Pura Luhur Uluwatu, is a beautiful temple located on top of a dramatic cliff. The temple is packed with monkeys, which are famous for stealing tourists’ stuff and demanding fruit in return.
A high-rated budget hostel in Ubud, very central to all the sites.
Honorable Mentions
Honorable Mentions
Enjoy a traditional Balinese massage for only IDR 350,000 or around $22 USD.
Zin@Work is a coworking space that started as a cafe, with healthy meals, an open-air bamboo workspace, a rooftop bar, a gym, and a spa. They offer a free trial with no time limit.
